verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin. However, there is substantial documented literature on HSP60 surface display on tumor cells and on stressed/apoptotic cells, where it acts as a danger signal and has been explored as an immunotherapy target (antibodies, CAR-T concepts targeting tumor-surface HSP60). This non-baseline ecto-pool under oncogenic/stress/cell-death states qualifies as cell_state_induced. Ruling out other buckets: not a lysosomal TM protein (no lysosomal_exocytosis); no narrow germline/developmental lineage restriction (not tissue_restricted_surface); no documented constitutive PM cycling pool at baseline (not dual_localization); no wash-resistant covalent TM-partner tethering described (not stable_surface_attachment).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of mitochondrial-to-surface translocation of HSP60 is not fully defined; surface pool magnitude varies across tumor contexts.
